mapper.select: SELECT o.order_id, o.order_no, p.product_name, o.order_status, o.order_deadline, o.order_quantity, mr.employee_id, o.difficulty_score, o.difficulty_grade FROM orders o JOIN product p ON o.product_id = p.product_id LEFT JOIN matching_record mr ON o.order_id = mr.order_id AND mr.matching_status = 'CONFIRM' WHERE o.order_id = #{orderId}

SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_count, COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'INPROGRESS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS in_progress_count, COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status != 'COMPLETED' AND order_deadline <= DATE_ADD(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 3 DAY) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS deadline_risk_count, ROUND( SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 1 ) AS achievement_rate, COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'REGISTERED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS registered_count, COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'ANALYZED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS analyzed_count, COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'COMPLETED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS completed_count FROM orders

SELECT o.order_id, o.v1_process_complexity, o.v2_quality_precision, o.v3_capacity_requirements, o.v4_space_time_urgency, o.alpha_novelty, o.difficulty_score, o.difficulty_grade, mr.employee_id AS recommended_technician_id FROM orders o LEFT JOIN matching_record mr ON o.order_id = mr.order_id AND mr.matching_status = 'CONFIRM' WHERE o.order_id = #{orderId}

SELECT o.order_id, o.order_no, p.product_name, o.order_status, o.order_deadline, mr.employee_id, e.employee_name, o.difficulty_score, o.difficulty_grade FROM orders o JOIN product p ON o.product_id = p.product_id LEFT JOIN matching_record mr ON o.order_id = mr.order_id AND mr.matching_status IN ('CONFIRM', 'INPROGRESS', 'COMPLETE') LEFT JOIN employee e ON mr.employee_id = e.employee_id WHERE o.order_deadline <= DATE_ADD(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 3 DAY) AND o.order_status != 'COMPLETED' ORDER BY o.order_deadline ASC

SELECT o.order_id, o.order_no, p.product_name, o.order_status, o.order_deadline, NULL AS employee_id, NULL AS employee_name, o.difficulty_score, o.difficulty_grade FROM orders o JOIN product p ON o.product_id = p.product_id WHERE o.order_status = 'ANALYZED' AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM matching_record mr WHERE mr.order_id = o.order_id AND mr.matching_status IN ('CONFIRM', 'INPROGRESS', 'COMPLETE') ) ORDER BY o.order_deadline ASC

SELECT COUNT(*) AS analyzed_order_count, ROUND(AVG(difficulty_score), 2) AS avg_difficulty_score, MAX(difficulty_grade) AS max_difficulty_grade FROM orders WHERE order_status IN ('ANALYZED', 'INPROGRESS', 'COMPLETED') AND difficulty_grade IS NOT NULL
